frameworks.name: multivariate encoding modeling
frameworks.one_line: fMRI voxel patterns analyzed via encoding models to read out feature codes

frameworks.name: hierarchical division-of-labor of cortical representation
frameworks.one_line: anterior regions hold abstract codes while primary sensory cortex holds detailed codes

frameworks.name: categorical coding of visual working memory
frameworks.one_line: extends categorical representation framework down into extrastriate cortex during WM

core_question: Are working memory representations of color categorical already at the level of extrastriate visual cortex, and do they differ from perception?
key_assumptions: voxel patterns reflect feature-selective neural representations
encoding models can dissociate categorical from continuous codes
subjects need not be cued to categorize for categorical codes to emerge
manipulations: task state (working memory delay vs perception)
regions of interest across the visual hierarchy (V4, VO1)
key_findings: categorical color codes present in extrastriate visual cortex (V4, VO1)
categorical coding observed during working memory but not during perception
categorical structure emerges without explicit or implicit categorization instructions
interpretations: visual working memory relies at least in part on categorical representations
abstract categorical codes are not exclusive to anterior cortex
